content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 7, 1990

Lance Olson
Olson, Connelly, Hagel, Fong & Leidigh
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA  95814

RE:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-028

Dear Mr. Olson:

You have requested advice on behalf of the California Dental
Hygienists' Association Political Action Committee (CAL-HY-PAC)
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1

QUESTION

Is CAL-HY-PAC required to maintain records on association
members whose annual dues earmarked for the PAC are less than $25
if the PAC knows the cumulative total for the calendar year from
individual members may exceed $25?

CONCLUSION

There is no requirement for CAL-HY-PAC to maintain records on
members whose annual dues earmarked for the PAC are less than $25
other than the date and total amount of such contributions, even
though the total amount contributed by certain members may exceed
$25 for the calendar year.

Although you indicated that no member contributes $100 or
more in a calendar year, if this situation changes please note
that even though the Political Reform Act does not require
detailed recordkeeping of any source which contributes less than
$25 at a time, if the committee knows, or has reason to know, that
any one source may contribute amounts under $25 totaling $100
during the calendar year, the purposes of the Political Reform Act
will be served if detailed records of such contributions are kept
and the total contributions itemized on the committee's campaign
statement.


1          Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Lance Olson
Page Two


FACTS

CAL-HY-PAC is the sponsored committee of the California
Dental Hygienists' Association. The PAC receives its
contributions through a system where members of the association
earmark a specific amount of their annual dues as contributions to
the PAC.

The amount of the annual earmarked payment usually is less
than $25. No individual member's total earmarked contributions
from annual dues equals or exceeds $100.

Occasionally, members are solicited for direct contributions
to the PAC in addition to the normal amount earmarked through
their dues payment.


ANALYSIS

Section 84104 requires treasurers of committees to "maintain
such detailed accounts, records, bills and receipts that are
necessary to prepare campaign statements...." Regulation 18401
clarifies that:

...accounts (are not required to) contain detail as to
contributions received of less than $25 other than the date
and total amount of such contributions; ...

If you have any additional questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant
